payload.document_title: Long Island Rail Road Financial and Ridership Reports – October 2023

warnings: source.document_title is accepted as passthrough, but prefer title. Document title label.

source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.
entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

tool_args.label: Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio YTD October 2023
Adjusted Cost Per Passenger YTD October 2023
Passenger Revenue Per Passenger YTD October 2023
Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio Month October 2023
Adjusted Cost Per Passenger Month October 2023
Passenger Revenue Per Passenger Month October 2023
Results preliminary and subject to adjustment

payload.gap_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. Please note that the current months' actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.

payload.raw_value_text: $98.8 million or 5.1% favorable to the forecast

tool_args.label: Primary drivers of favorable expense variance

payload.claim_text: The primary drivers of favorable expense variance are vacant positions and associated fringe costs, OPEB Current, electric power expenses, the timing of pension payments, material usage, maintenance and other operating contracts, and professional services contracts, partially offset by higher overtime, fuel expenses, the timing of claims, FELA, and other business expenses.
payload.subject: LIRR expense variance

tool_args.label: Farebox Revenue favorable due to higher ridership

payload.claim_text: Farebox Revenues were $8.0 million favorable to the forecast due to higher ridership, partially offset by lower yield per passenger.
payload.subject: LIRR farebox revenue

payload.claim_text: The vacancies were primarily in Transportation, Program Management, and various administrative departments.
payload.subject: LIRR staffing vacancies

tool_args.label: Labor Expenses favorable due to vacant positions

payload.claim_text: Payroll expenses were $15.9 million favorable (primarily due to vacant positions). Health & Welfare expenses were $8.7 million favorable (vacant positions). OPEB Current Payments were $10.9 million favorable (fewer retirees/beneficiaries than projected).
payload.subject: LIRR labor expenses

payload.claim_text: Unfavorable non-reimbursable overtime was primarily driven by higher programmatic/routine maintenance, vacancy/absentee coverage, and unscheduled maintenance, partially offset by scheduled service and unscheduled service.
payload.subject: LIRR overtime

tool_args.label: Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio methodology note

payload.claim_text: Adjusted Fare Box Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators have been adjusted for comparability between the Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad and include: Inclusion of Other Operating Revenue, Removal of OPEB Current Payment expenses for retirees, and Removal of the UAAL associated with the LIRR's closed pension plan.
payload.subject: LIRR performance indicators

tool_args.label: LIRR has metric: Total Revenue YTD
LIRR has metric: Total Ridership YTD
LIRR has metric: Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio YTD
LIRR has metric: Adjusted Cost Per Passenger YTD
LIRR has claim about expense variance drivers

payload.claim_text: Through October 2023, ridership was 53.5 million, 24.4% above 2022 and (29.6)% below 2019 pre-COVID levels (adjusted for the same number of workdays).
payload.subject: LIRR ridership comparison to 2019 pre-COVID

tool_args.label: GCMCOOC expenses included in LIRR performance indicators

payload.claim_text: Expenses for Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company (GCMCOOC) are included in LIRR performance indicator calculations.
payload.subject: LIRR performance indicators
